hypothesis: If frozen molecular LLMs are steered by adding weighted concept-embedding vectors learned via a Concept Bottleneck Model to their intermediate activations, then they will generate molecules whose properties (e.g., QED, LogP) more closely match specified targets (lower MAE/Best100MAE) while maintaining validity, novelty, uniqueness, and synthetic accessibility, outperforming activation-add baselines and rivaling or exceeding fine-tuning methods even on out-of-distribution property targets.
